title: contacts_search / contacts_list can hang MCP clients on large stores
description: ContactsBridge is synchronous (Contacts framework, not async). searchByEmail enumerates the entire contact store client-side with no predicate pushdown; listContacts without a group does the same. On large stores or slow Contacts.app state this blocks MCP clients indefinitely. Apply the Mail/Notes Outcome<T> + soft-timeout pattern from dd7b487 / 6bb02ba, but use a Swift wall-clock check inside enumerateContacts (Contacts framework doesn't support JXA-style script injection). Default softTimeoutMs=22000, clamp [1000, 300000]. Emit warnings via OutputOptions.emit(...,timedOut:,timedOutHint:). Ships in v0.20.2.
close_reason: Shipped in v0.20.2. ContactsBridge.listContacts (no group) and searchByEmail now return Outcome<[ContactInfo]> with wall-clock soft timeout (22s default, clamped [1s, 5min] via shared SoftTimeout.clamp). ContactsCommand threads outcome.timedOut through output.emit(). Tag pushed, CI green, tap updated to v0.20.2, brew upgrade succeeded, make install refreshed ~/.local/bin shadow.

title: Cut v0.20.1 release (mail+notes MCP timeout fixes)
description: Five commits landed on main since v0.20.0 (mail/notes latency bounding + beads hygiene) but are untagged. Cut v0.20.1 to ship them to Homebrew/MCP users. Follows docs/skills/release/SKILL.md. Plan at ~/.claude/plans/1-and-2-twinkling-floyd.md Part A.
close_reason: v0.20.1 shipped: tag pushed, CI green, tap updated, brew upgrade verified (Cellar/pippin/0.20.1), ~/.local/bin shadow refreshed via make install (Installed: /Users/matthewwagner/.local/bin/pippin (0.20.1)).

title: mail_activity times out in MCP clients — 120s script timeout exceeds 60s MCP cap
description:
mcp__pippin__mail_activity hangs past the 60s MCP runChild hard cap on every call, reported 3 consecutive evenings (2026-04-21/22/23) from the wfm-evening-priority-brief scheduled task. Reproduced with `{since: 2026-04-22, limit: 30, preview: 300}` and inside batch.

Root cause (verified in pippin/MailBridge/MailBridge.swift:36 and MailBridgeScripts.swift:303):
1. listActivity uses ScriptRunner timeoutSeconds=120 when preview>0 — **exceeds** the 60s MCP runChild hard cap in pippin/MCP/MCPServerRuntime.swift. The child process is reliably SIGTERM-killed before the JXA script returns, so MCP callers always get the childTimedOut error after exactly 60s.
2. buildActivityScript has no internal time check. It walks every target mailbox (default [INBOX, Sent]) per account, scans up to 500 msgs each, dedups, sorts, then for the preview pass calls `results[p].__msg.content()` per survivor — another N IMAP body fetches on iCloud. No early-break.
3. No ActivityOutcome / timedOut surfacing path.

Fix (apply the pippin-v1e pattern):
- buildActivityScript: accept softTimeoutMs (default ~45000 since activity does 2+ mailboxes; clamp 1s–5min). Guard the outer accounts × targetNames × messages loop AND the post-slice preview loop. Emit `{results, meta: {…, timedOut}}` instead of bare array.
- MailBridge.listActivity: return ActivityOutcome { messages, timedOut }. Lower ScriptRunner timeout to 55s (below the MCP 60s cap, above the 45s soft cap).
- MailActivityCommand: reuse/mirror MailCommand.Search.emitResult helpers — stderr warning, text trailer, envelope warnings.
- Tests: ActivityMetaTests, backward-compat decode, script-builder snippet assertions.

Secondary: even without the soft-timeout, lowering the ScriptRunner timeout below 60s is a standalone bug fix — right now every preview-on activity call is guaranteed to be killed by MCP runChild.
acceptance_criteria:
- mcp__pippin__mail_activity returns within ~50s on a 2-mailbox iCloud account instead of hitting the 60s MCP cap.
- When soft cap fires, envelope includes `warnings: [...]` and partial results under `.data`.
- ScriptRunner timeout strictly less than MCP runChild hard cap (60s).
- Full test suite green.
notes: Reference implementation: pippin-v1e (7bb7c54) + refactor 6bb02ba. MCP tool registered at pippin/MCP/ToolRegistry.swift:194. MailActivityCommand at pippin/Commands/MailActivityCommand.swift.

title: mail_list times out in MCP clients — apply pippin-v1e soft-timeout pattern
description:
mcp__pippin__mail_list hangs past the 60s MCP runChild hard cap on every call, reported 3 consecutive evenings (2026-04-21/22/23) from the wfm-evening-priority-brief scheduled task. Reproduced with `{limit: 20, preview: 150}` and with no args. mail_search (fixed via pippin-v1e) returns fine; mail_list and mail_activity (this bug + pippin-NEXT) still hang.

Root cause (verified in pippin/MailBridge/MailBridge.swift:21 and MailBridgeScripts.swift:6):
1. buildListScript has no internal time check — it loops every account/mailbox/message with msg.content() when preview>0 (forces per-message IMAP body fetch).
2. listMessages uses ScriptRunner timeoutSeconds=60 when preview>0 (10s otherwise). The 60s matches the MCP runChild hard cap (pippin/MCP/MCPServerRuntime.swift), so any slow mailbox hits the cap and returns zero data.
3. No partial-results SearchOutcome analogue — no way to return what was collected + a timedOut warning.

Fix (apply the exact pippin-v1e pattern):
- buildListScript: accept softTimeoutMs (default ~18000, clamped 1s–5min). Wrap the outer accounts/messages loop with an early-break on `Date.now() - _listStart > softTimeoutMs`, set `_meta.timedOut = true`, emit `{results, meta}` instead of a bare array.
- MailBridge.listMessages: return a ListOutcome { messages, timedOut } (or reuse SearchOutcome shape). Lower ScriptRunner timeout to 30s so soft cap fires first.
- MailCommand.List: surface timedOut via the same emitResult/emitMessages/emitPage helpers introduced in 6bb02ba — stderr warning, text trailer, envelope-level `warnings: [...]` in agent mode.
- Tests: mirror MailBridgeSearchMetaTests + JXAScriptBuilderTests — backward-compat decode for old bare-array responses, script-builder snippet assertions.
acceptance_criteria:
- mcp__pippin__mail_list returns within ~25s on large iCloud inboxes instead of hitting the 60s MCP cap.
- When soft cap fires, agent envelope includes `warnings: ["mail list timed out; results may be incomplete"]` and partial results under `.data`.
- text mode: stderr warning + trailer when --verbose.
- Existing non-preview callers unaffected (10s path unchanged).
- Full test suite green.
notes: Reference implementation: commit 7bb7c54 (pippin-v1e) + 6bb02ba refactor. Reporter: automated scheduled task wfm-evening-priority-brief. See bug report in session history 2026-04-23. MCP tool registered at pippin/MCP/ToolRegistry.swift:172.

title: Fix mail_search MCP timeout (soft cap + MCP runChild hard timeout)
description: mcp__pippin__mail_search times out from MCP clients because (1) ScriptRunner timeout is 90s, exceeding typical MCP client 30s window; (2) MCP runChild has no timeout, can hang forever if subprocess wedges. Plan: 22s JXA soft timeout returning partial results with meta.timedOut, lower ScriptRunner timeout 90→30, add 60s hard timeout to MCPServerRuntime.runChild.
close_reason: Soft 22s JXA timeout + 30s ScriptRunner cap + 60s MCP runChild hard timeout. Search now returns partial results with envelope warnings instead of hanging past MCP client timeout. Verified mail search 'WFM' returns in 22.1s. 1499 tests pass.

title: mail_search returns empty + mail_show times out on Gmail
description: mail_search scans only top-level mailboxes via acct.mailboxes(), missing Gmail's nested [Gmail]/All Mail, Sent, Trash, etc. Result: empty arrays for queries that Apple Mail finds 66 results for. Separately, readMessage uses default 10s timeout; large messages with attachments need more. See /Users/matthewwagner/Desktop/cowork/projects/WFM_Onboarding/pippin_mail_bug_report.md
close_reason: Fixed: search now recurses nested mailboxes (Gmail [Gmail]/All Mail, etc.) via jsCollectAllMailboxes helper with Message-ID dedup; per-mailbox scan cap raised 200→500; search timeout 60s→90s; mail show readMessage timeout 10s→45s. 1101 tests pass.

title: mail attachments: -10000 / -1728 errors — wrong mailbox + unsafe save target
description:
pippin mail attachments <compound-id> fails on Gmail accounts in Mail.app for two distinct reasons:

1) **Wrong mailbox lookup (-1728).** The compound id format 'account||mailbox||num' embeds the Gmail *label* (e.g. 'Important', 'Starred'). pippin's AppleScript does 'mailbox "Important" of account <acc>' which errors -1728 because Gmail labels aren't addressable as mailboxes in Mail.app — the actual on-disk mailbox is INBOX. Fix: if the initial mailbox lookup returns -1728, fall back to scanning 'every message of mailbox "INBOX" of account <acc>' and filter by id/message-id/sender.

2) **Save target not pre-created (-10000).** Even once the message is found, 'save <mail attachment> in POSIX file "/path/to/file"' errors with 'Can't get POSIX file …' unless the target file already exists. Fix: before the save call, 'do shell script "touch" & quoted form of targetPath' (or the Swift equivalent). This is what finally unblocked a real extraction run on 2026-04-19.

**Evidence.** Reproduced against 'matthewwagner038@gmail.com||Important||6442'. 'pippin mail show' on the same compound id works fine (body + headers returned), so the issue is specifically in the attachments sub-command's AppleScript construction. Workaround (direct osascript) successfully extracted 16 attachments from the Shternie Bulua thread on 2026-04-19. Full workaround and the working script shape is documented at ~/.openclaw/workspace/MAIL_ATTACHMENTS_WORKAROUND.md.

**Scope.**
- Ensure mailbox resolution degrades to INBOX (or iterates mailboxes) when the label-as-mailbox lookup fails.
- Pre-touch the save target file before the AppleScript 'save' call.
- Add a regression test against a Gmail account with a labeled message that has >=1 attachment.
close_reason: Fixed in fe45558: nested-mailbox fallback + pre-touch + JXA 'in:' key + mimeType() guard + source() for IMAP binaries. Verified against the reported repro (matthewwagner038@gmail.com||Important||6442 — 198k PDF saved). Regression tests added in JXAScriptBuilderTests.

title: Central remediation-hint layer for error output
description: No command currently suggests 'pippin doctor' when it would help. Only InitCommand.swift references that string. AgentOutput.swift has printAgentError but doesn't enrich with remediation. Add a Remediation catalog keyed by error category, wire it through printAgentError and the non-agent error path so typed errors always carry actionable next steps.
acceptance_criteria: 1. New pippin/Formatting/Remediation.swift maps error category codes (fda_denied, missing_dep_mlx_audio, mlx_audio_version_mismatch, ollama_unreachable, ...) to {humanHint, doctorCheck, shellCommand?}. 2. AgentOutput.printAgentError emits a remediation field when the error maps to a known category. 3. pippin-entry/Pippin.swift's non-agent error path appends the human hint + 'Run pippin doctor for diagnostics.' to stderr. 4. At least 3 error categories covered at ship time.
close_reason: Closed

title: Pre-flight FDA check for memos subcommands
description: pippin memos list currently surfaces raw 'SQLite error 23: authorization denied' when Full Disk Access is missing, with no remediation hint. DoctorCommand.swift:182-236 already has the exact check needed but no other code calls it. Extract into a reusable VoiceMemosDB.checkAccess() helper and call it at the top of every memos subcommand run().
acceptance_criteria: 1. VoiceMemosDB has static checkAccess() helper. 2. All MemosCommand subcommands (List/Info/Export/Transcribe/Delete/Summarize) call it at top of run(). 3. Typed MemosAccessError.fdaRequired carries System Settings remediation + 'Run pippin doctor for diagnostics'. 4. Agent-mode error JSON includes code:'fda_denied' and remediation field. 5. Unit test covers access-check failure path.
close_reason: Closed

title: Browser needs NODE_PATH to find globally installed Playwright
description: pippin browser commands fail unless NODE_PATH=/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules is set. Node.js cannot resolve globally-installed playwright without this env var. pippin doctor reports Playwright as found (checks binary existence) but the actual require() fails at runtime. Fix options: (1) set NODE_PATH in the spawned node process, (2) use npx playwright internally, (3) resolve the module path at doctor-check time and pass it explicitly.
close_reason: Closed

title: Contacts search crashes
description: pippin contacts search crashes while pippin contacts list works fine. Discovered during real-world testing on Pi agent (Talia). Needs investigation — likely an issue in CNContactStore query construction or result handling in the search code path. Reproduce: pippin contacts search "<any name>"
close_reason: Closed

title: Browser snapshot fails: page object undefined in persistent context
description:
## Bug

`pippin browser snapshot` crashes with:
```
Error: Browser script failed: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'snapshot')
```

## Root Cause

In `BrowserBridge.swift:buildSnapshotScript()`:

```javascript
const browser = await webkit.launchPersistentContext(session, { headless: true });
const page = browser.pages()[0] || await browser.newPage();
const snapshot = await page.accessibility.snapshot({ interestingOnly: false });
```

**Problem:** When snapshot is called on an existing session:
1. browser.pages()[0] may be undefined (no pages in context)
2. await browser.newPage() may not work correctly with persistent context
3. page remains undefined → crash on page.accessibility.snapshot()

## Reproduction

```bash
pippin browser open "https://example.com"
pippin browser snapshot  # Crashes
```

## Proposed Fix

Check if page exists and has valid URL before snapshotting. If no page exists, either:
- Return error 'No page loaded'
- Or open about:blank first before snapshotting
close_reason: Closed

title: REPL interactive mode
description: Add a 'pippin shell' REPL mode inspired by CLI-Anything. When invoked with no subcommand, drop into an interactive session with command history, auto-suggest, context-aware prompt showing active account/mailbox, and session state. Agent-friendly: deterministic prompt, JSON output mode, composable within a single session.
close_reason: Closed

title: Notes JXA: pre-loop fetch+sort can exceed ScriptRunner cap on large vaults
description: In NotesBridge.buildSearchScript and buildListScript, app.notes() (or folder.notes()) + .slice().sort() runs BEFORE the soft-timeout-protected loop. On vaults with hundreds of notes this fetch+sort alone can take 30s+, hitting the ScriptRunner 30s hard cap before the loop's soft cap can fire. Result: user gets typed NotesBridgeError.timeout instead of the friendlier 'partial results' warning, and zero data is returned. Fix sketch: hoist the time check in front of the sort, so if pre-loop work alone exceeds softTimeoutMs we set _meta.timedOut=true and return empty results with the warning. Reference: confirmed via manual repro on a 440-note vault — 'pippin notes search test --limit 3 --format agent' returns timeout error, not soft-timeout warning.

title: Notes list: add JXA soft timeout (mail_search analogue)
description: NotesBridge.listNotes fetches all notes, sorts by modificationDate, then calls .body() + .plaintext() on each in the slice. No internal time check; large vaults can exceed MCP timeout. Add 22s soft timeout inside the .map over sliced notes. Wrap response in {results, meta:{timedOut}}.
close_reason: Shipped in dd7b487: NotesBridge searchNotes/listNotes/listFolders return Outcome<T> with 22s soft timeout in JXA loop; surfaced via shared OutputOptions.emit() helper to stderr/agent/text. Mail-search analogue applied across all three Notes JXA loops.

title: Notes search: add JXA soft timeout (mail_search analogue)
description: NotesBridge.searchNotes iterates every note in the folder/app, calls .plaintext() per note, and has no internal time check. Identical shape to the recently-fixed mail_search bug. Add 22s soft timeout inside the JXA loop, return Outcome { result, timedOut }, surface meta.timedOut in the agent envelope warnings.
close_reason: Shipped in dd7b487: NotesBridge searchNotes/listNotes/listFolders return Outcome<T> with 22s soft timeout in JXA loop; surfaced via shared OutputOptions.emit() helper to stderr/agent/text. Mail-search analogue applied across all three Notes JXA loops.

title: Fix process.waitUntilExit() blocking cooperative thread in DoctorCommand and AudioBridge
description:
DoctorCommand.run() and AudioCommand.run() are declared async throws. They call process.waitUntilExit() directly on the cooperative thread pool, blocking threads for seconds to minutes.

DoctorCommand: checkNotesAutomation() (~line 335), python3-check helper (~line 460)
AudioBridge: runProcess() (~line 407)

Fix: Wrap Process.waitUntilExit() in withCheckedThrowingContinuation + DispatchQueue.global().async, or mark helpers @concurrent (Swift 6.2).

Severity: High — blocks cooperative threads under any concurrent usage

title: Audit OllamaEmbeddingProvider: embed/embedBatch block cooperative thread via DispatchSemaphore
description:
OllamaEmbeddingProvider.embed and embedBatch are synchronous functions that internally call sendSynchronousRequest() which uses DispatchSemaphore.wait() with a 125s timeout.

Currently only called correctly via withCheckedThrowingContinuation + DispatchQueue.global().async in MailIndex, but the type contract doesn't enforce this. Any future async call site that forgets the GCD hop will block a cooperative thread for up to 125s.

Fix: Make embed/embedBatch async throws using URLSession.data(for:), or mark them @concurrent (Swift 6.2) to make the executor contract explicit.

File: pippin/MailAIBridge/EmbeddingProvider.swift, AIProvider/AIProvider.swift

title: AI-firstify polish: split CLAUDE.md gotchas, ship release skill, prune docs
description:
Audit under /ai-firstify skill flagged three small gaps:
A. CLAUDE.md packs ~40 inline gotchas — move to docs/gotchas/*.md for progressive disclosure
B. Release workflow is a skill candidate — ship as docs/skills/release/SKILL.md, symlink from .claude/skills/ locally (.claude/ is gitignored)
C. Review/prune docs/archive/ and docs/agent-prompts/ for staleness
Low risk, reversible, improves session context efficiency for every future agent.
close_reason: CLAUDE.md split into progressive-disclosure gotchas (295->191 lines); release procedure shipped as docs/skills/release/SKILL.md with make link-skills target; docs/README.md index added

title: reminders_list needs created-after / modified-after recency filter
description:
Cowork feedback 2026-04-20: 'Reminders list has no recency filter. The full list came back with items going back to 2024, all mixed together. A createdAfter or modifiedAfter filter would make it much easier to focus on what is actually new or recently changed during an update scan.'

Current reminders_list (pippin/MCP/ToolRegistry.swift:366) supports: list, completed, dueBefore, dueAfter, priority, limit. No filter by creation or modification date.

EKReminder exposes .creationDate (already used for sort at pippin/RemindersBridge/RemindersBridge.swift:98) and .lastModifiedDate — both available for filtering.

Add CLI flags --created-after / --modified-after (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO 8601) to 'pippin reminders list' and surface them in the reminders_list MCP tool schema. Accept a relative shortcut like '7d' if the date-parse helper already supports it.

Acceptance: 'pippin reminders list --modified-after 2026-04-13' returns only reminders touched in the last week.
close_reason: Added --created-after and --modified-after to reminders list (CLI + MCP tool) filtering on EKReminder.creationDate / .lastModifiedDate. Mirrored existing dueBefore/dueAfter validation. Committed in 04bcf2e on branch claude/exciting-goldwasser-87467d.

title: mail_list returns metadata only — add body preview or optional body inclusion
description:
Cowork feedback 2026-04-20: 'Mail returns metadata only, no body. I could see subjects and senders but couldn't read content without fetching each email individually. For a comprehensive scan trying to surface action items and commitments, that is a significant limitation — I would need a lot of extra tool calls to actually read anything.'

Current shape: mail_list (pippin/MCP/ToolRegistry.swift:172) returns MailMessage metadata. To read bodies, the agent must call mail_show per message — expensive for scans.

Proposals (pick one):
1. Add --preview N flag to mail list that returns first N chars of plain text body per message.
2. Add --with-body flag that includes full body (slow; caller opt-in).
3. Add a new mail_scan tool that returns subject + sender + body preview in a single call, optimized for agent scan workflows.

Note: mail_search already has --body, but that is a search predicate, not a return-body flag.

Acceptance: an agent doing a 'scan my inbox for commitments' task can do it in one tool call per mailbox, not N+1.
close_reason: Added --preview N flag to mail list (CLI + mail_list MCP tool). bodyPreview is opt-in (script unchanged when flag absent); when set, msg.content() triggers IMAP fetch, truncated with ellipsis at min(N, 4000). Timeout bumps to 60s with preview on. MailMessage.bodyPreview added as encodeIfPresent (back-compatible). Committed 4bebb0b (squash-merged in 0d827e0).

title: Auto-launch fallback for Apple apps when bridge times out
description:
Cowork feedback 2026-04-20: notes_list failed with 'Notes automation timed out — ensure Notes.app is running.' Notes was a blind spot for a comprehensive scan. User requested this fallback apply to ALL Apple-app-backed services (Notes, Mail, Calendar, Reminders, Contacts), not just Notes — any bridge that depends on a host app being running should auto-launch it (via 'open -a <AppName>' or equivalent) and retry once before surfacing the timeout error.

Candidate implementation: shared helper that wraps JXA/subprocess bridges. On timeout or 'application isn't running' style errors, invoke 'open -gja <App>' (background, activate) then re-run the script once. If it still fails, return the current error. Apps to cover: Notes.app, Mail.app, Calendar.app, Reminders.app, Contacts.app. Music.app/Podcasts not currently bridged.

Ref: pippin/NotesBridge/NotesBridge.swift:295 runScript, pippin/Models/NoteModels.swift:13 timeout message.
close_reason: Extracted shared ScriptRunner from NotesBridge/MailBridge; adds open -gja retry once on timeout when appName is provided. Scope narrowed to Notes+Mail (Calendar/Reminders/Contacts use EventKit/CNContactStore, no host app dep). 5 ScriptRunner tests + existing 38 Notes/Mail tests pass. Committed 4609088 (squash-merged in 0d827e0).

title: Register memos tools in MCP ToolRegistry
description: pippin/MCP/ToolRegistry.swift registers 26 tools but excludes all memos subcommands per docs/mcp-server.md's 'heavy AI subsystems out of scope' policy. That's the wrong call for agentic use — memos is precisely what Claude agents want to reach. Register 5 memos tools that shell out to the existing --format agent CLI paths.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ToolRegistry.swift registers memos_list, memos_info, memos_export, memos_summarize, memos_transcribe. 2. Each tool's argv ends in --format agent (enforced by existing testAllArgvEndWithFormatAgent lint test). 3. docs/mcp-server.md drops the blanket memos exclusion and documents the 5 new tools. 4. tools/list response over stdio includes the new tools.
close_reason: Landed in pippin v0.18.0 (commits 535d021, eb99fd8, 872a64c) and homebrew-tap 33b7883. All six acceptance criteria met; 1098 tests green; doctor smoke verified on M4.

title: Fix mlx-audio invocation + pin version
description: pippin invokes mlx-audio via 'python3 -m mlx_audio.stt' in AudioBridge.swift:64. mlx-audio 0.4.2 broke this by restructuring the module, producing 'No module named mlx_audio.stt.__main__'. Pippin has no pinned version anywhere. Switch to the entry-point binary with version-aware fallback, pin a known-good version, and have DoctorCommand report version mismatch cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: 1. AudioBridge.swift resolves and invokes mlx_audio.stt.generate entry point (not -m module path) when available; falls back to -m only for older versions. 2. MLXAudioTranscriber.pinnedVersion constant documents the known-good version. 3. DoctorCommand reports installed vs pinned version and runs a dry invocation. 4. Version-mismatch surfaces as typed error with clear remediation, not a cryptic module error.
close_reason: Landed in pippin v0.18.0 (commits 535d021, eb99fd8, 872a64c) and homebrew-tap 33b7883. All six acceptance criteria met; 1098 tests green; doctor smoke verified on M4.

title: Mail search fails with TypeError on null allMsgs
description: pippin mail search crashes with: 'execution error: TypeError: null is not an object (evaluating allMsgs.length)'. Observed during Pi agent testing when searching for messages. The JXA script's allMsgs variable is null when the mailbox cannot be accessed or hasn't loaded. Need a null guard in the generated JXA search script.
close_reason: Closed

title: Doctor mlx-audio check doesn't find pipx installs
description: pippin doctor checks for mlx-audio via system Python but mlx-audio is commonly installed via pipx, which uses its own venv. Doctor reports FAIL even when mlx-audio is installed and functional via pipx. Should also check pipx list or common pipx paths.
close_reason: Closed

title: Unified daily digest command
description: Add pippin digest that combines mail summary, calendar agenda, due reminders, and recent notes into a single structured briefing. Replaces the need to call 4 separate commands in the morning briefing task. Supports --format agent for token-efficient output.
close_reason: Implemented DigestCommand with mail/calendar/reminders/notes sections, DigestPayload model, NoteDigestInfo, MCP tool registration, 22 new tests. All 1105 tests pass.

title: Calendar conflict detection
description: Add pippin calendar conflicts --from --to that finds overlapping events. Also integrate into smart-create: warn (or abort in --dry-run) if the proposed event overlaps an existing one. Agent mode returns structured conflict details.
close_reason: Implemented CalendarConflict model, findConflicts bridge, Conflicts subcommand, --allow-conflicts SmartCreate guard, 23 new tests

title: REPL tab completion
description: Add tab completion in the REPL for command names, subcommands, flag names, and contextual values (e.g. mail account names, reminder list names). Use libedit/readline integration or a custom completer. Makes the interactive shell much more usable.
close_reason: Implemented 'complete <partial-input>' built-in REPL command with ReplCompleter providing suggestions for commands, subcommands, and flags. Tested with various completion scenarios.

title: Contacts create/edit/delete
description: Contacts bridge is currently read-only. Add write support: pippin contacts create --first 'Alice' --last 'Smith' --email alice@example.com, pippin contacts edit <id> --phone '+1234567890', pippin contacts delete <id> --force. Uses CNMutableContact + CNSaveRequest.
close_reason: Closed

title: Watch mode for real-time mail notifications
description: Add pippin mail watch that polls for new messages and emits events as newline-delimited JSON. Useful for agent workflows that need to react to incoming mail in real-time. Options: --account, --interval (default 30s), --mailbox.
close_reason: Implemented pippin mail watch — NDJSON polling with --account/--mailbox/--interval/--limit. Seeds seen-ID set on startup. Commits 9c3407b + ce6f194 on main.

title: Reminders natural language create
description: Add pippin reminders smart-create '<natural language>' — similar to calendar smart-create. AI parses due date, priority, list assignment from freeform text. e.g. 'remind me to call the dentist next Tuesday at 9am priority high'.
close_reason: pippin reminders smart-create fully implemented in prior session

title: Mail triage rules engine
description: Let users define persistent triage rules in ~/.config/pippin/triage-rules.json — e.g. auto-label, auto-archive, priority overrides based on sender/subject/keywords. pippin mail triage would apply rules before (or instead of) the AI pass. Reduces token usage for predictable patterns.
close_reason: Implemented TriageRulesEngine with loadRules/apply, TriageRuleModels, MailTriage --no-rules/--rules-file flags, 23 tests all passing.

title: MCP server mode
description: Add a pippin mcp-server subcommand that exposes pippin's capabilities as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. Each command group becomes a tool — mail_list, calendar_today, reminders_create, etc. This lets any MCP-compatible client (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.) use pippin natively without shelling out. Use stdin/stdout JSON-RPC transport.
close_reason: Implemented mcp-server subcommand with 26 tools, stdio JSON-RPC, subprocess dispatch. 1074 tests pass (25 new). End-to-end smoke tested: init/tools/list/tools/call (status, calendar_today, reminders_create, reminders_complete, error paths). Docs in docs/mcp-server.md.

title: pippin status introspection command
description: Add 'pippin status' that shows a dashboard: configured mail accounts, mailbox counts, pending reminders, today's calendar events, memos storage stats, system permissions. Enables agents to probe before mutating — the inspect-before-act pattern from CLI-Anything.
close_reason: Closed

title: SKILL.md agent discovery file
description: Generate a machine-readable SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description, triggers) and structured body (command groups, usage examples, JSON output examples). Follows CLI-Anything's standard schema so Pippin is discoverable by any agent framework — OpenClaw, Claude Code skills, CLI-Hub.
close_reason: Closed

title: Perf: batch embedding requests and fix concurrency in index + search
description:
Three targeted performance improvements to mail AI subsystem:
1. OllamaEmbeddingProvider: add embedBatch() that sends all texts in a single /api/embed request — reduces N round-trips to 1 during index
2. MailAICommand index Phase 2: use embedBatch instead of spawning unlimited concurrent tasks
3. SemanticSearch: parallelize final message loading using DispatchGroup
4. Fix runBatchesConcurrently semaphore ordering — rateLimiter.wait() blocks dispatcher thread before task is queued
close_reason: Implemented batch embedding, concurrent message loading, and early-exit on error

title: vault-scan false positives: Google Fonts URLs
description:
The vault-scan tool flags Google Fonts CDN URLs as 'Basic Auth URL' findings due to the @ symbol in font parameter strings.

**Affected files:** Design reference HTML files in Prevention Coach project

**Example false positive:**
https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Material+Symbols+Outlined:wght,FILL@100..700,0..1&display=swap

The @ in font specs (e.g., wght,FILL@100..700) triggers the Basic Auth URL pattern.

**Recommended fixes:**
1. Add exclusion pattern for fonts.googleapis.com URLs in vault-scan
2. Or improve Basic Auth URL detection to require actual username:password format before @

**Files affected:** 6 HTML files in design references (not production code)
close_reason: Fixed: tightened Basic Auth URL regex in vault-scan from '://[^[:space:]]+:[^[:space:]]+@' to '://[^@/:[:space:]]+:[^@/:[:space:]]+@[^/:[:space:]]' — requires actual user:pass@host format, no longer matches query params with @ like Google Fonts URLs

title: Add async withTimeout wrapper for EventKit bridges (Reminders, Calendar)
description: Reminders + Calendar bridges are EventKit-backed and async — hang risk is low in practice (EventKit predicates are efficient). Deferred from the v0.20.1/v0.20.2 MCP-latency pass. Revisit IF a real hang is reported against reminders_list, calendar_events, or findConflicts on a large vault/wide date range. Then: add a generic withAsyncTimeout helper (Swift Task.timeout / TaskGroup race pattern), thread softTimeoutMs through, and reuse OutputOptions.emit(...,timedOut:,...) for the warning envelope. Captured so the skipped scope isn't lost.

title: Lift Outcome<T> into a shared bridge module
description: Outcome<T> (results + timedOut flag, back-compat decoder) lives in pippin/NotesBridge/NotesBridge.swift. Once ContactsBridge gains its own copy (pippin-hlw), consider extracting to pippin/Formatting/BridgeOutcome.swift (or similar shared location) so future bridges reuse a single definition. Non-urgent; duplication is 30 lines per bridge, and premature consolidation risks coupling unrelated bridges. Revisit if a third bridge picks up the pattern.
notes: Partial progress in v0.20.2: shared SoftTimeout enum now centralizes defaultMs + clamp, which covered an adjacent duplication found during the simplify pass. The Outcome<T> struct itself remains duplicated (NotesBridge.Outcome has a custom Decodable init for JXA JSON; ContactsBridge.Outcome is pure Swift no-Decodable). Lifting those into a shared module still requires resolving that shape mismatch — e.g. split into OutcomeCore<T> + DecodableOutcome<T>, or just leave them separate. Revisit when a 3rd bridge adopts the pattern.

title: pippin doctor: add mail_list/mail_activity/mail_search latency probes
description:
Bug report 2026-04-23 suggested step #1 was "Run `pippin doctor` and check mail-specific permissions and index status" — today `pippin doctor` does not probe mail-bridge latency or surface whether Mail.app is ready. After pippin-kis + pippin-tl8 land, add per-tool timing probes so future hangs are diagnosable without reading source.

Probes to add:
- Mail.app ready poll round-trip time (jsMailReadyPoll latency)
- `mail list --limit 1` cold-start time
- `mail activity --since <now-1h> --limit 1` cold-start time  
- `mail search "" --limit 1` cold-start time (uses new soft-timeout path)

Flag as yellow if any probe exceeds 20s, red if exceeds 55s (would fail MCP clients).
notes: Depends on pippin-kis and pippin-tl8 landing so the probes don't themselves hang. Related: pippin-5et (AIProvider MCP-friendly timeout).

title: Surface Notes Outcome.timedOut in ActionsCommand/DigestCommand/StatusCommand
description: ActionsCommand.collectNoteItems, DigestCommand notes section, and StatusCommand.gatherNotesStatus all call NotesBridge.listNotes()/listFolders() and discard the new Outcome.timedOut flag via .results. Result: morning digest could silently include partial Notes data with no advisory; status counts could under-report. Surface the flag: digest should add a 'notes' entry to its top-level warnings array; actions should propagate it on its envelope; status should include a 'notes_timed_out' boolean in StatusReport.NotesStatus. Out of scope of the original Notes timeout PR (file pippin-u2z/o5w/22p) because it requires touching unrelated payload schemas.

title: AIProvider URLSession timeout: add MCP-friendly variant for Ollama/Claude
description: OllamaProvider/ClaudeProvider use a hardcoded 120s URLSession timeout. That's correct for CLI use (you want to wait for a slow local model) but exceeds the 60s runChild MCP cap, so MCP clients see 'pippin child exceeded 60s and was terminated' instead of 'AI request timed out / model unreachable'. Options: (a) lower for MCP-invoked path only via a mode signal, (b) add a fast preflight ping to Ollama (HEAD /api/tags) before the actual completion call to fail fast when Ollama is down, (c) document the trade-off and accept it. Out of scope for the Notes timeout PR.

title: Voice Memos batch ops (export/list --transcribe): add wall-clock soft cap
description: memos export --all --transcribe and memos list --transcribe --all chew through up to 10000 memos with no per-batch wall-clock cap. Will trigger the 60s MCP runChild hard cap mid-batch with no partial-result return. Needs a soft cap inside BatchCommand parallel job scheduling that returns whatever was completed + a warning advising to narrow the query (e.g. --since, smaller --limit). Out of scope for the Notes timeout PR — file separately because the fix touches parallel job aggregation.

title: Notes folders: add JXA soft timeout (mail_search analogue)
description: NotesBridge.listFolders enumerates every folder, calls .notes().length per folder. Nested unbounded enumeration with no time check. Add 22s soft timeout at the top of the folders loop. Wrap response in {results, meta:{timedOut}}.
close_reason: Shipped in dd7b487: NotesBridge searchNotes/listNotes/listFolders return Outcome<T> with 22s soft timeout in JXA loop; surfaced via shared OutputOptions.emit() helper to stderr/agent/text. Mail-search analogue applied across all three Notes JXA loops.

title: Fix XCTest unavailable in CLT SDK on macOS 26 (swift test fails)
description:
Running 'swift test' (via 'make test') fails with 'no such module XCTest' in AIProviderTests.swift when using the Command Line Tools SDK on macOS 26 (macosx26.4). XCTest.framework is not present in /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk on macOS 26.

Workaround: run with DEVELOPER_DIR=/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer swift test.

Fix: Update Makefile to use xcrun --sdk macosx swift test, or add XCTest search path to Package.swift, or add a note to CLAUDE.md.

The CI workflow (runs on macos-15) is unaffected.

title: Fix DispatchSemaphore.wait() blocking cooperative thread in StatusCommand
description:
StatusCommand.run() is async throws. It calls buildStatusReport() → gatherRemindersStatus(), which calls sem.wait(timeout: .now() + .seconds(5)) on the cooperative thread. This blocks a Swift cooperative thread for up to 5s, potentially starving other tasks.

Fix: Wrap the EKEventStore.fetchReminders call with withCheckedContinuation to make gatherRemindersStatus async and deliver the result via continuation.resume().

File: pippin/Commands/StatusCommand.swift ~line 176
Severity: Medium (CLI tool, rarely concurrent)

title: Mail activity scan: combined INBOX + Sent pass for commitment tracking
description:
Cowork feedback 2026-04-20: 'No sent mail scan. I only looked at INBOX. A proper activity scan should also check sent mail for commitments you have made (I will send that by Friday type things). That would need an explicit second call with mailbox: Sent.'

mail_list already accepts a mailbox arg, so the agent can call it twice — the issue is discoverability and efficiency. Two approaches:

1. Tool description nudge: update mail_list description (pippin/MCP/ToolRegistry.swift:173) to explicitly mention agents should also query mailbox=Sent for activity scans. Cheap.

2. New mail_activity / mail_scan tool: single call returns recent messages across INBOX + Sent (and optionally Drafts) merged and sorted, with body previews. Pairs with the mail_list body-preview feature (separate issue). Ideal for morning-briefing / daily-scan workflows.

Prefer (2) since it also fixes the N+1 problem and gives Cowork/morning-briefing a single natural entry point. Depends on the mail body-preview feature.
close_reason: Shipped mail activity subcommand + MCP tool. Combined INBOX+Sent scan with body preview, dedup by messageId, alias resolution with collectAllMailboxes fallback. 25 new tests, all 1149 tests passing. Committed aee4e33+55fda62 (squash-merged in 0d827e0).

title: Unify FDA remediation text between DoctorCommand and RemediationCatalog
description: DoctorCommand.checkVoiceMemosDB() has its own free-form FDA remediation string on DiagnosticCheck; RemediationCatalog has another. They say the same thing but in different words. Refactor so DiagnosticCheck.remediation is backed by a Remediation struct (or pulled from RemediationCatalog by code) — one source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: 1. DiagnosticCheck.remediation is either typed as Remediation? or populated from RemediationCatalog by error code. 2. No duplicated FDA text in the repo (grep 'Full Disk Access' returns one canonical home). 3. Doctor output in text mode reads the same or better than before. 4. Doctor output in JSON mode carries the structured remediation (new key).
close_reason: Landed in pippin v0.18.0 (commits 535d021, eb99fd8, 872a64c) and homebrew-tap 33b7883. All six acceptance criteria met; 1098 tests green; doctor smoke verified on M4.

title: Type-safe error category enum for RemediationCatalog keys
description: RemediationCatalog.forCode keys on raw strings ('access_denied', etc.) that must match agentErrorCode(for:) output exactly. A typo in either place silently breaks the lookup. Wrap the codes in a closed enum (ErrorCategory: String or similar) and thread it through AgentError + RemediationCatalog + call sites so the compiler enforces the coupling.
acceptance_criteria: 1. New enum ErrorCategory (or similar closed type) replaces string keys in RemediationCatalog. 2. AgentError and RemediationCatalog share the same key type — can't drift. 3. Existing snake_case JSON output unchanged (agent consumers see no schema change). 4. Unit tests for all existing categories still pass.
close_reason: Landed in pippin v0.18.0 (commits 535d021, eb99fd8, 872a64c) and homebrew-tap 33b7883. All six acceptance criteria met; 1098 tests green; doctor smoke verified on M4.

title: Homebrew postinstall for mlx-audio transcription deps
description: Users installing via brew install pippin currently hit a manual 'pip install mlx-audio' step before memos transcribe works. Extend the Homebrew tap formula to run pipx install mlx-audio==<pinned> as a post_install, and teach AudioBridge to resolve the pipx-installed binary path. Cross-repo work: formula lives in mattwag05/homebrew-tap.
acceptance_criteria: 1. /opt/homebrew/Library/Taps/mattwag05/homebrew-tap/Formula/pippin.rb has a post_install block that runs 'pipx install mlx-audio==<pinned>'. 2. Skip or advise cleanly when pipx is absent. 3. AudioBridge.swift probes for the binary via which + pipx environment, with -m as final fallback. 4. README.md install section documents the new flow. 5. Verified with brew uninstall && brew install mattwag05/tap/pippin on a clean state.
close_reason: Landed in pippin v0.18.0 (commits 535d021, eb99fd8, 872a64c) and homebrew-tap 33b7883. All six acceptance criteria met; 1098 tests green; doctor smoke verified on M4.

title: AVFoundation audio conversion before transcribe
description: VoiceMemosDB.transcribeMemo() passes source audio paths straight through to the STT backend with no format normalization. When Voice Memos files aren't in a format the backend can handle natively, transcription fails. Add a Swift-native (AVFoundation-based) converter that normalizes to 16 kHz mono WAV before passing to the backend.
acceptance_criteria: 1. New pippin/MemosBridge/AudioConverter.swift implements AVAssetExportSession/AVAudioFile conversion to 16kHz mono WAV. 2. VoiceMemosDB.transcribeMemo() calls converter when source extension isn't in the native set (.wav, .m4a). 3. Converted files land under NSTemporaryDirectory() and are cleaned up after transcription. 4. --keep-converted debug flag preserves the temp file and prints its path.
close_reason: Landed in pippin v0.18.0 (commits 535d021, eb99fd8, 872a64c) and homebrew-tap 33b7883. All six acceptance criteria met; 1098 tests green; doctor smoke verified on M4.

title: Session state persistence
description: Add session state management: remember active account/mailbox context, draft state, operation history with undo capability. Persist to ~/.config/pippin/session.json with file locking. Primarily useful once REPL mode exists — session state carries across commands within a REPL session.
close_reason: Closed

title: Universal --json on all commands
description: Audit and add --format json support to doctor, init, completions, and any other subcommands missing it. Every command should produce deterministic, parseable JSON output when --json is passed. Consistent error format across all commands.
close_reason: Closed

title: Refactor: extract runConcurrently helper shared by TriageEngine and SemanticSearch
description: TriageEngine.runBatchesConcurrently and the concurrent dispatch pattern in SemanticSearch.search() are near-identical (DispatchGroup + NSLock + index-sorted results). Extract a generic runConcurrently<Input, Output> free function in a new ConcurrencyUtils.swift file in MailAIBridge/. SemanticSearch can use it directly; TriageEngine can wrap it for its batch semantics. Eliminates ~12 lines of duplication and unifies error handling.
close_reason: Closed

value: pippin mail_search was fixed via pippin-v1e (soft-timeout JXA pattern + MCP runChild 60s hard cap) on 2026-04-22. mail_list and mail_activity did NOT get the same fix — listMessages uses ScriptRunner 60s/10s timeout, listActivity uses 120s/30s. Both have no JXA internal time check, no partial-results flag. mail_activity's 120s script timeout exceeds the MCP runChild 60s hard cap, so it's reliably killed.

ContactsBridge soft-timeout pattern (v0.20.2): wall-clock deadline inside enumerateContacts via stop.pointee=true. Uses shared SoftTimeout.clamp(_:) + SoftTimeout.defaultMs (22000ms). Outcome<T> stays per-bridge — NotesBridge.Outcome has custom Decodable init for JXA JSON, ContactsBridge.Outcome is pure Swift. EventKit bridges (Reminders/Calendar) deliberately NOT bounded yet — see pippin-91n.
